spec_match.rationale: Reach-measurement is the exact use case the chromestatus entry cites as the reason for shipping; the page models lookbackWindow filtering and a single histogram contribution via Private Aggregation, which is the canonical algorithm.

interactive.rationale: Slider for the lookback window (days converted live to ms) plus a campaign-tag select drive a real table re-render and a recomputed contribution count; no static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises the lookbackWindow + campaign filter then a single aggregate; page delivers exactly that with the contribution sentence updating per change.

edge_cases.rationale: Mock dataset includes IGs that match neither filter, but doesn't explicitly demo (a) zero matches → no contribution, (b) the lookbackWindow=0 (just-joined) boundary, or (c) IGs with no userBiddingSignals.tags — the worklet code does .tags?.includes but the visible fixtures all have tags.

references.rationale: see-also links the Turtledove reach-measurement explainer and chromestatus.com/feature/5074536530444288.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, sliders use accent-color: var(--text-black), in-window vs out-window rows use bg-paper / bg-stone with charcoal text so contrast is fine.

openQuestions.title: Document the lookbackWindow=0 edge case
openQuestions.detail: A user who joined an IG within the last poll interval has lookbackWindow ~ 0. Add a fixture row with lookbackDays: 0 and explain in the summary that the filter is strictly less-than, so even the 'just joined' IG counts as long as max > 0. Right now the slider starts at 30 and no fixture is < 1.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'no contribution' state to the summary
openQuestions.detail: When the user filters to zero matching IGs the contribution sentence still says 'Worklet would contribute 0' — wire a visible 'no aggregate emitted' banner so the privacy property (no contribution = no report) is obvious, since that's the whole point of the API in this context.

openQuestions.title: Compare to the pre-v134 reach-measurement workaround
openQuestions.detail: Before this feature, reach measurement had to be approximated with sharedStorage.append over time, which is much noisier. A two-column 'before / after' card on the page (or a sibling concept) would make the win concrete instead of implicit.

openQuestions.title: Note the contribution budget constraint
openQuestions.detail: Private Aggregation has a per-context contribution budget (the other feature shipping in v134 — see private-aggregation-api-per-context-contribution-limits-for-shared-storage-calle). The worklet code listing should at least comment that budget exhaustion is a failure mode here.

summary: Strong concept: the slider+campaign filter exposes exactly the algorithm chromestatus cites as the motivating use case, and the simulated worklet keeps the algorithm runnable even without Privacy Sandbox flags. Edge cases (zero matches, lookback=0, budget exhaustion) are the only gaps.
